evidence.claim: Used Hugging Face Transformers, NLP, Recommendation Systems, FAISS, Reinforcement Learning, CSS, LlamaIndex, TTS, Qdrant, BM25, Sentence Transformers, Semantic Search, Object Detection, Learning to Rank, LangChain, and Prompt Engineering.
evidence.source_text: Skills: Hugging Face Transformers... NLP... Recommendation Systems... FAISS... Reinforcement Learning... CSS... LlamaIndex... TTS... Qdrant... BM25... Sentence Transformers... Semantic Search... Object Detection... Learning to Rank... LangChain... Prompt Engineering

evidence.claim: Evolved e-commerce search ranking from hand-tuned scoring to learning-to-rank model.
evidence.source_text: Owned the ranking layer for an e-commerce search product, evolving it from a hand-tuned scoring function to a learning-to-rank model

evidence.claim: Developed semantic search for ~500K documents using sentence-transformers and FAISS.
evidence.source_text: Developed a semantic search feature for an internal knowledge base of ~500K documents. Used sentence-transformers... with FAISS

evidence.claim: Improved revenue-per-search by 12% via learning-to-rank model.
Improved search-relevance by 35% over Elasticsearch BM25.
evidence.source_text: Reported search-relevance improvement of 35% over the prior Elasticsearch BM25 setup

evidence.claim: Led the team that migrated keyword-search-based product to embedding-based retrieval.
evidence.source_text: Recently, I led the team that migrated our keyword-search-based product to embedding-based retrieval.

evidence.claim: Earned Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer certification.
evidence.source_text: Certifications: Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer (Google Cloud, 2022)

evidence.claim: Prioritizes practical experience over academic theory for system reliability.
evidence.source_text: My academic background is in CS/ML but my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.

evidence.claim: Focuses on infrastructure and data quality over modeling.
evidence.source_text: Most of the work was infrastructure and data quality — the modeling part was almost the easy bit.
